exampleSentences: Khi anh đến thành phố mới, anh đã lạc lối trong khu phố cổ.
Nếu em không có bản đồ, em rất dễ bị lạc lối ở đây.
Chị ấy cảm thấy lạc lối khi không biết nên làm gì tiếp theo.

translation.en: I have been lost in this city.

content.sentence: Tôi đã ___ trong thành phố này.

notes_md:
**Lạc lối** means “lost.” It can be physical (you don’t know where you are) or emotional/mental (you don’t know what to do in life).

It works like a state adjective after **bị** or **cảm thấy**. You can also use it after verbs of movement or in situations where someone lacks direction.


Close synonym: **lạc đường** (more physical, about roads). For emotional meaning, it’s similar to **mất phương hướng** (stronger, more serious).
exampleSentencesTranslated: When he came to the new city, he was lost in the old quarter.
If you don't have a map, you can easily get lost here.
She felt lost when she didn't know what to do next.

definitions.en: To be lost or disoriented, unable to find one's way.
definitions.vi: Bị lạc hoặc không định hướng, không thể tìm đường.
definitions.es: Estar perdido o desorientado, incapaz de encontrar el camino.

example.en: I feel lost in this city.
example.es: Me siento perdido en esta ciudad.
example.vi: Tôi cảm thấy lạc lối trong thành phố này.

translation.en: I feel lost on this road.

content.sentence: Tôi cảm thấy ___ trên con đường này.

notes:
Lạc lối means “lost.” It can be physical (you don’t know where you are) or emotional/mental (you don’t know what to do in life).

It works like a state adjective after bị or cảm thấy. You can also use it after verbs of movement or in situations where someone lacks direction.

Close synonym: lạc đường (more physical, about roads). For emotional meaning, it’s similar to mất phương hướng (stronger, more serious).
